faqs.question: Why do I have silverfish in my East Elmhurst home?
faqs.answer: Silverfish are attracted to moisture and humidity, which are common in East Elmhurst due to its proximity to Flushing Bay and the airport. They often enter through cracks, feed on paper products, starches, and fabrics, and thrive in bathrooms, basements, kitchens, and attics with poor ventilation.
